case_title: RICKY SHADDOX VS. RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRA
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY PETITIONER SHADDOX, RICKY AS TO RESPONDENT RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRA NCISCO JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - JUDGE BUSCH DISCLOSED THAT HIS WIFE, CATHARINE BARNES, IS A DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY. SHE IS ASSIGNED TO THE CONTRACTS TEAM. SHE DOES NOT NORMALLY HANDLE LITIGATION OR POLICE OR PENSION MATTERS, AND SHE HAS HAD NO INVOLVEMENT OF ANY KIND IN THIS LITIGATION. JUDGE BUSCH DOES NOT VIEW HER EMPLOYMENT AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 29, 2013 PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES AND BY THAT TIME THE PETITIONER'S RENOTICED MOTION TO SUBSTITUTE THE PROPER PARTY OR SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST HAS ALREADY BEEN DECIDED BY THE COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 = (302/PJB)
ASSOCIATION OF ATTORNEYS: LARSON, KYLE R. ADDED AS ATTORNEY FOR SHADDOX, RICKY
CLERK'S NOTICE TO COUNSEL RE DESIGNATION OF RECORD ON APPEAL
REPLY BRIEF FILED BY PETITIONER SHADDOX, RICKY
FEE PAID FOR A MOTION FILED ON JANUARY 23, 2013 FILED BY PETITIONER SHADDOX, RICKY
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-29-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER RICKY SHADDOX'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. RESPONDENT PROPERLY INTERPRETED SF CHARTER A8.597-3. THE WCAB DETERMINES THE "PERCENTAGE OF DISABILITY" THAT FORMED THE BASIS FOR RESPONDENT'S DETERMINATION THAT THE MEMBER WAS INCAPACITATED AND ENTITLED TO INDUSTRIAL DISABILITY RETIREMENT. ALLOWING INJURIES OR INCAPACITIES THAT WERE RAISED IN THE FIRST INSTANCE BEFORE THE WCAB TO PROVIDE A BASIS FOR THE RETIREMENT DETERMINATION AND BENEFIT CALCULATION WOULD INFRINGE UPON RESPONDENT'S PLENARY POWERS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976

calendar.matters:
Mtn For Writ Of Mandamus; Ntc Of Lodging
Off Calendar Apr-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: KYLE R. LARSON, ESQ., 415.729.5953, FOR PETITIONER RICKY SHADDOX.
NANCY J. TAVERNIT, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3800, FOR RESPONDENT RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PETITIONER RICKY SHADDOX'S PETITION For Writ Of Mandamus is denied. Respondent properly interpreted SF Charter A8.597-3. The WCAB determines the "percentage of disability" that formed the basis for respondent's determination that the member was incapacitated and entitled to industrial disability retirement. Allowing injuries or incapacities that were raised in the first instance before the WCAB to provide a basis for the retirement determination and benefit calculation would infringe upon Respondent's plenary powers. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

Mtn For Writ Of Mandamus; Ntc Of Lodging
Off Calendar Mar-29-2013 Continued To Apr-12-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. The petition For Writ Of Mandamus is continued to April 12, 2013 because Judge Goldsmith is hearing the law and motion calendar and he recuses himself. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

ATTY. NANCY J. TAVERNIT, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3961 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - Judge Busch disclosed that his wife, Catharine Barnes, is a deputy city attorney. She is assigned to the contracts team. She does not normally handle litigation or police or pension matters, and she has had no involvement of any kind in this litigation. Judge Busch does not view her employment as a basis for recusal. No objections were made. The matter was discussed. Motion for writ of Mandamus is continued to March 29, 2013 per agreement of the parties and by that time the Petitioner's renoticed motion to substitute the proper party or successor in interest has already been decided by the court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 = (302/PJB)
